hero.tagline: Taree's local workshop — all makes, all models
hero.subtext: Mechanical repairs, logbook servicing, pink and blue slips, AC regas — everything your car needs, at one address in Taree. We quote before we start and call before we spend a dollar more.

aircon.heading: We can legally regas your AC. Most shops can't.
aircon.subheading: Australia requires an ARCtick licence to handle automotive refrigerant. Corney's holds one. If your AC isn't cold, we're the closest workshop in Taree that can actually fix it — not just refer you somewhere else.
aircon.priceSub: Standard passenger vehicle · All-inclusive · No surprises
aircon.leakTestSub: If your system has lost gas, we find the leak first — no point regassing a system that'll just lose it again.
points: Licensed to handle all automotive refrigerants (R134a, R1234yf)
Proper recovery, evacuation and recharge — not a top-up
Leak diagnosis with UV dye or nitrogen pressure test
Full component repair (compressor, condenser, expansion valve)

whyUs.heading: Workshop work done the right way — every single time.
whyUs.subtext: Four reasons Taree drivers have kept coming back for 20 years.

points.title: A Quote Before We Lift a Spanner
points.desc: We diagnose, we call, we quote. Then you decide. No $200 service that mysteriously becomes $800 at pickup.

points.title: Licensed for the Jobs Most Can't Touch
points.desc: ARCtick for AC work. AUVIS for blue slips. NSW authorised for pink slips and defect clearances. One stop — instead of three.

points.title: Warranty-Safe Logbook Servicing
points.desc: Serviced to manufacturer specs, stamped by the book, recorded properly. Your new-car warranty stays exactly where it should.

points.title: The Mechanics Work on Your Car
points.desc: Jarrad, Don and Toby — the three names on the shop wall — are the three people holding the spanners. No call centre, no apprentice-only jobs, no finger-pointing.

howItWorks.heading: Three steps. No stress.
howItWorks.subtext: From booking to keys-in-hand, here's exactly how it works.

steps.title: Book It In
steps.desc: Call (02) 6552 6131, send a message, or drop by. We'll lock in a time that suits — usually same week, often sooner.

steps.title: We Diagnose & Quote
steps.desc: Drop your car at 16 Albert Lane. We check it over, call you with a clear quote before starting, and only do what you've approved.

steps.title: Keys Back, Job Done
steps.desc: We message when it's ready. Clear rundown of what was done, why, and what the bill was — matching the quote.

testimonials.heading: What Taree drivers say.
testimonials.subtext: Five stars on Google. Here's why.

reviews.name: Sarah M.
reviews.text: Booked in for a logbook service, they called before doing anything extra — found a cracked rear shock but gave me the option. Done by 4pm, quote matched the bill to the dollar. That's rare.

reviews.name: Dave R.
reviews.text: Front brakes started grinding on a Monday morning. Rang Corney's at 8am, they fit me in by 10, had new pads and machined rotors back to me same day. Proper job, fair price, no drama.

reviews.name: Michelle T.
reviews.text: AC had been weak for ages. Every other shop wanted to just 'top it up' — Corney's did a proper leak test, found a bad O-ring on the condenser, fixed it and regassed. Ice cold since. $189 regas plus the repair — no upsell, no games.

reviews.name: Tom K.
reviews.text: Pink slip done in under an hour. They flagged a CV boot starting to split — not a fail, but warned me before it became one. That's the kind of heads-up you only get from a mechanic who's not trying to fleece you.

reviews.name: Karen B.
reviews.text: New-car warranty means I had to stick to the logbook. Corney's used the right oil, the right filter, stamped the book, emailed me the invoice. Warranty intact, half the price of the dealer in Newcastle.

reviews.name: James L.
reviews.text: Interstate plates, rego expired. Needed a blue slip before I could re-register. Corney's are an AUVIS station so it was one visit instead of two. Straight, fast, sorted.
